articles.title: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
articles.raw_content: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.
articles.summary: Etsy, plataforma de artesanía, ahora está inundada de productos masivos y copias de inteligencia artificial.

articles.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
articles.raw_content: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.
articles.summary: La aprobación final resuelve un caso, pero no aborda la cuestión más amplia de utilizar obras con derechos de autor para entrenar modelos de inteligencia artificial.

articles.title: From experiment to expectation: BAI on delivering private 5G across Australian industry
articles.raw_content: Q&A with Chris Upstone, Director of Private and Public Networks, BAI Communications Private 5G networks are rapidly moving from experiment to expectation across Australian industry. As deployments grow in scale…
articles.summary: BAI Communications impulsa redes 5G privadas en la industria australiana, pasando de experimentos a expectativas.

articles.title: India's first privately-developed rocket reaches orbit on dramatic debut launch
articles.raw_content:
Indian space officials celebrated the debut flight of Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket, India's first fully commercial satellite launcher, as a "grand success" Saturday after an on-target climb into a 280-mile-high orbit following liftoff from an island spaceport in the Bay of Bengal.

The Vikram-1 lifted off from India's primary spaceport on Sriharikota Island at 1:35 am EDT (06:35 UTC) Saturday, around midday at the launch base along India's southeast coast. The launch was delayed more than a half-hour to resolve a last-minute technical problem. The countdown resumed, culminating in the command to ignite Vikram-1's solid-fueled first stage booster to propel the rocket off the launch pad.

Vikram-1 is modest in size compared to India's larger workhorse rockets. Skyroot's rocket stands about 72 feet (22 meters) tall, with the capability to place payloads of up to 770 pounds (350 kilograms) into low-Earth orbit. This makes Vikram-1 somewhat larger than the Electron launch vehicle developed by Rocket Lab, the world's most successful dedicated small satellite launcher.

Read full article

Comments
articles.summary: La empresa india Skyroot Aerospace lanza con éxito su cohete Vikram-1, el primer lanzador de satélites comercial del país.

articles.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
articles.raw_content: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.
articles.summary: Se debate si la demanda de Apple afectará los planes de OpenAI para entrar en el hardware y salir a bolsa.
